title: '@Autowired' should be used when multiple constructors are provided
description: In Spring, when a class defines multiple constructors, it is necessary to explicitly annotate one of them with '@Autowired' to indicate which constructor should be used for dependency injection. Failing to do so can lead to ambiguity and runtime errors.
recommendation: Always annotate one constructor with '@Autowired' when a class has more than one constructor to ensure Spring knows which one to use for dependency injection.
impact: Ambiguity in constructor selection may cause application startup failures or unexpected behavior.

checks.message: Always annotate one constructor with '@Autowired' when a class has more than one constructor to ensure Spring knows which one to use for dependency injection.
